label.reasoning: The issue describes a failure in the expected behavior where an SMS is sent but the corresponding data is not stored, indicating a bug in the backend processing or database interaction.

duplicates.title: Auto sms functionality is not working
duplicates.body: when i made the payment i am getting the sms but the entry is not getting store in the app

repo_context.code:
selection.append(" AND ${Telephony.Sms.BODY} LIKE ?")
            selectionArgs.add("%$keyword%")
        }

        val cursor = try {
            applicationContext.contentResolver.query(
                Telephony.Sms.CONTENT_URI,
                arrayOf(Telephony.Sms.BODY, Telephony.Sms.DATE, Telephony.Sms.ADDRESS),
                selection.toString(),
                selectionArgs.toTypedArray(),
                "${Telephony.Sms.DATE} ASC"
            )
        } catch (e: Exception) {
            Log.e("SmsBackfill", "Query failed", e)
            null
        }

        val transactionsToInsert = mutableListOf<Transaction>()
        val importLogs = mutableListOf<String>()
        var processedSmsCount = 0
        var foundTransactionsCount = 0
        val totalSms = cursor?.count ?: 0

        cursor?.use {
            val bodyIndex = it.getColumnIndex(Telephony.Sms.BODY)
            val dateIndex = it.getColumnIndex(Telephony.Sms.DATE)
            val addressIndex = it.getColumnIndex(Telephony.Sms.ADDRESS)

            while (it.moveToNext()) {
                val body = it.getString(bodyIndex)
                val date = it.getLong(dateIndex)
                val address = it.getString(addressIndex)

                SmsParser.parse(body, date, address, accounts, categories, forcedAccountId)?.let { tx ->
                    transactionsToInsert.add(tx)
                    val logEntry = "${tx.category}: ₹${AmountFormatter.format(tx.amount)} (${SimpleDateFormat("dd MMM", Locale.getDefault()).format(Date(tx.date))})"
                    importLogs.add(logEntry)
                    foundTransactionsCount++
                }

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<manifest xmlns:android="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res/android"
    xmlns:tools="http://schemas.android.com/tools">

    <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.RECEIVE_SMS" />
    <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.READ_SMS" />

    <application
        android:allowBackup="true"
        android:dataExtractionRules="@xml/data_extraction_rules"
        android:fullBackupContent="@xml/backup_rules"
        android:icon="@mipmap/ic_launcher"
        android:label="@string/app_name"
        android:roundIcon="@mipmap/ic_launcher_round"
        android:supportsRtl="true"
        android:theme="@style/Theme.BudgetApp">
        
        <receiver
            android:name=".SmsReceiver"
            android:exported="true"
            android:permission="android.permission.BROADCAST_SMS">
            <intent-filter>
                <action android:name="android.provider.Telephony.SMS_RECEIVED" />
            </intent-filter>
        </receiver>

        <activity
            android:name=".MainActivity"
            android:exported="true"
            android:label="@string/app_name"
            android:theme="@style/Theme.BudgetApp">
            <intent-filter>
                <action android:name="android.intent.action.MAIN" />

                <category android:name="android.intent.category.LAUNCHER" />
            </intent-filter>
        </activity>
    </application>

</manifest>

analysis.root_cause_summary: The most likely root cause is that the `SmsParser.parse()` method (not provided in the context) is failing to extract transaction details from the received SMS body, causing it to return `null`. When `SmsParser.parse()` returns `null`, the `transactionDao.insertTransactionWithReconciliation(tx)` block is never executed, and no transaction is logged or stored.
analysis.reasoning: The issue states that SMS messages are received, but entries are not stored. `SmsReceiver.kt` is the component responsible for real-time SMS processing and storage. Inside `SmsReceiver.kt`, SMS messages are parsed by `SmsParser.parse()`. If this method returns `null`, the subsequent `.let { tx -> ... }` block, which contains the database insertion logic, will not execute. The existing logging only covers successful insertions or exceptions during the database operation itself, not when parsing fails to yield a transaction object. Adding a log for null parsing results will confirm this theory.
analysis.fix_strategy: Add a log statement in `SmsReceiver.kt` to explicitly record when `SmsParser.parse()` returns `null`. This will help diagnose whether the SMS parsing logic is the point of failure.
